paper.title: Runaway OB Stars in the Small Magellanic Cloud III. Updated Kinematics and Insights on Dynamical vs. Supernova Ejections

extraction.summary: This paper studies runaway and walkaway OB/OBe stars in the Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC). All objects are discussed as ejected from their parent clusters within the SMC (DES/BSS mechanisms), not as candidates unbound from the Milky Way. No Galactic-unbound or escaping HVS candidates are presented.

candidate_groups_considered.description: SMC field OB and OBe stars with kinematic data (336 stars).
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents these objects as runaway/walkaway stars ejected from clusters within the Small Magellanic Cloud, not as candidates possibly unbound from the Milky Way Galaxy.

source_refs.context: Abstract states the work concerns field OB and OBe stars in the Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC), not the Milky Way.
Method section describes transverse velocities calculated relative to local systemic field motion within the SMC.
Section defines runaways and walkaways as stars unbound from their clusters with v_perp >= 24 km/s, within the SMC context.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Subpopulation kinematics summary for SMC OB/OBe stars, HMXBs, EBs, and SB2s.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Aggregate kinematic statistics for SMC subpopulations. No individual objects are presented as Milky-Way-unbound candidates.

source_refs.context: Table 2 caption and data present median transverse velocities for SMC subgroups, with no mention of Galactic escape.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Numbers and ratios of walkaway and runaway field OB stars in the SMC.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Statistical ratios of walkaways to runaways among SMC field stars. These are cluster-ejection population statistics, not Galactic-unbound candidate objects.

source_refs.context: Table 3 presents DES/BSS runaway and walkaway ratios for SMC field stars, not Galactic HVS candidates.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Model runaway and walkaway frequencies for DES and BSS ejections in the SMC.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Model predictions for cluster ejection frequencies within the SMC. No individual objects or Galactic-unbound candidates are discussed.

source_refs.context: Table 4 shows model frequencies of runaways and walkaways from cluster ejections in the SMC, with no connection to Milky Way escape.
